milestone_brief: Close the deferred F-M2 + F-L2 findings from display-toggles-checkable-button-2026q3-e1. Two coordinated layout improvements in the Appearance panel: (a) text-align consistency across the Colors and Display groups (currently the Display group's QPushButton(checkable=True) toggles for Wireframe / Show edges / HQ smoothing have setProperty('role', 'display-toggle') + QSS text-align: left from the v1 critic's F-M2; the Colors group's Surface… / Background… QPushButton instances inherit Qt's default center-alignment — creating a visible vertical-rhythm fracture across the group boundary on narrow panels ~200px); (b) the Display group header is generic — Blender uses 'Overlay' or 'Shading', 3D Slicer uses 'Display Type', ParaView uses 'Representation' — propose a more specific label like 'Render Mode' or 'Surface Style'. Research the right combined approach: either (1) extend text-align:left to the global QPushButton rule (broadest fix; might regress Reset Defaults / Reset Camera which are intentionally outside the role-property pattern) or (2) add a 'colors-button' role to Surface… / Background… so they pick up the same left-align treatment as display-toggle WITHOUT cascading to all QPushButton, OR (3) re-evaluate whether the display-toggles should actually be center-aligned to match Colors (the reverse fix). Researcher should evaluate visual rhythm by looking at the rendered panel chrome (use the existing .claude/scripts/frontend-uplift/render-panel-chrome.py captures), and pick the option that best matches the established scientific-viz convention (Blender/Slicer/ParaView). For the header rename, audit Blender / 3D Slicer / ParaView terminology and propose. AI invariants: AI-2 (tests stay Qt-free), AI-11 (any new role-property strings are pure data — no enum exposure), AI-13 (no new color tokens if reusing the established role-property pattern). Surface preservation: the actual click behavior of Surface… / Background… (color picker) and the display toggles (wireframe state, edges state, HQ state) MUST NOT change — this is a pure visual-alignment + header-label pass.
research_synthesis: Option 2: setProperty('role', 'colors-button') on surf_btn + bg_btn in _build_color_group; new QPushButton[role=colors-button] QSS rule with text-align: left + padding/border-radius to force QStyleSheetStyle on macOS. QGroupBox('Display') → QGroupBox('Render Mode') per MeshLab peer convention. ~19 LOC across appearance_panel.py + styles.py. No signal/slot/color/icon changes. 3 new source-grep tests (AI-2 compliant).
